title: Newswire mobile story detail separating sourced facts, AI summary, context, and publishers
productContext: Fictional global finance-news mobile app (product name 'Newswire') for iOS and Android. The story-detail surface is the highest-stakes screen in the app: it is where a reader goes deep on a single market event and where the line between what a publisher actually reported and what Newswire's machine-generated AI summary said must be unambiguous. Newswire is reader-and-aggregation software only; it does not employ analysts, does not issue its own reporting, and does not publish price targets, ratings, or buy/sell recommendations. The 'AI summary' is a machine-generated condensation of publisher articles; the 'context' panel is machine-assembled background (prior coverage, related tickers, geography) and is never editorial. The app is pre-scale: there is no published accuracy metric, no source-authority ranking, and no claim that any publisher is more reliable than another. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: investigative reader who wants to read the publisher's own words first and only then consult the AI summary and context
fact-checker verifying which publisher made which specific claim and whether the AI summary faithfully reflects the sources
casual reader who wants the gist fast but must not be led to believe the AI summary is the publisher's verdict or a source of record
jobs: read the sourced publisher article in full before, or instead of, the AI summary, with the source identity and timestamp always visible

tell apart, at a glance and at every scroll position, four distinct layers: sourced publisher text, machine-generated AI summary, machine-assembled context, and other publishers covering the same story
trace any AI-summary sentence back to the specific publisher article(s) it condenses, and flag when a summary point is not backed by a visible source
consult context (prior coverage, related tickers, geography) knowing it is machine-assembled, not an editorial narrative or a price recommendation
requiredJourneys: investigative reader opens a story, reads the sourced publisher article first, and reaches the AI summary only afterwards with the source identity still visible
fact-checker expands the AI summary, taps a summary sentence, and lands on the specific publisher passage it condenses, with an honest 'no source backs this point' affordance when none exists
casual reader gets the gist from the AI summary but the screen never lets the summary masquerade as the publisher's verdict or a source of record
any reader scrolls from sourced text through AI summary and context into the other-publishers list without the four layers ever blurring into one another
constraints: CRITICAL: The four content layers — sourced publisher text, machine-generated AI summary, machine-assembled context, and other publishers — must be visually and semantically separated at all times; they must never merge into a single Newswire-authored narrative, and each layer must carry a persistent label identifying what it is.

The AI summary must be labelled machine-generated and attributed to its source publisher(s) on every sentence or paragraph; do not present it as the publisher's verdict, Newswire's reporting, an editorial conclusion, or a source of record. Every summary point must link to the source passage it condenses.

Where an AI-summary point cannot be traced to a visible source passage, the screen must surface an explicit 'no source backs this point' affordance rather than presenting the point as established fact; do not silently fill gaps with confident-sounding synthesis.
Do not display any AI-accuracy percentage, confidence score, source-authority ranking, or 'most reliable source' verdict; Newswire ranks neither the AI's correctness nor one publisher over another.

The context panel (prior coverage, related tickers, geography) must be labelled machine-assembled and must not read as an editorial narrative, a price forecast, or a buy/sell recommendation; do not present context as Newswire's analysis.

Do not fabricate analyst quotes, price targets, ratings, subscriber counts, read counts, or any metric not supplied in this brief; the publisher's own words are the only authoritative content on the screen.
Source identity and timestamp must remain visible on sourced publisher text at every scroll position; the reader must never lose track of which publisher said what and when.
The other-publishers list must present each publisher neutrally with its own timestamp and headline, with no implied reliability ranking or 'best source' marker.
The product name Newswire is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, rating agency, or data vendor.

mobileRules: the publisher name, headline, and timestamp remain sticky or persistently visible while the sourced article body scrolls

the article-unavailable and paywall-detected states keep the AI summary and context reachable but never substitute the AI summary for the missing publisher text without an explicit substitution notice
each summary sentence or paragraph keeps its machine-generated label and its source link tappable in a single-handed grip
the no-source-for-point affordance stays inline with the sentence it refers to and is never collapsed into a generic disclaimer
the context panel keeps a 'machine-assembled, not editorial analysis' label visible whenever any context item is shown
related tickers and geography tags are tappable but never read as price recommendations or forecasts
each publisher row shows its own headline and timestamp and carries no reliability ranking or 'best source' marker
the single-publisher-only state names honestly that only one publisher covers the story so far and offers a 'get notified when more arrive' path without implying Newswire is suppressing sources
